claim: The moderator gives the participant a double task: dispute a $48.00 charge and lower the online purchase limit to $50 a month
quote: Today's task: you got a notification about a charge of $48.00 at an electronics store that you didn't make. You need to dispute this transaction and also temporarily lower the limit on online purchases for the card to $50 per month

claim: The participant's first action is tapping the balance on the main screen, expecting a list of transactions
quote: Tapping on the balance.

claim: Before acting, the participant verbalizes the expectation that tapping the balance will show the transaction list
quote: Well, I think there'll be a list of recent transactions, that's usually how banks do it.

claim: Snag 1: tapping the balance produces no visible result
quote: Hm. Nothing happened? Or maybe that was just an animation.

claim: Snag 2: the transaction card has no dispute button, only "Repeat Payment"
quote: At the bottom of the card there's only one button — "Repeat Payment." Why would I repeat a payment I never made?

claim: Snag 3: the three-dot menu on the card does not contain a report/dispute function
quote: A menu popped up: "Add to Favorites," "Export Receipt," "Share." Not it again.

claim: Snag 4: the "Dispute" function is only reachable via a swipe on the list row, not on the card
quote: Oh! There it is, it appeared — "Dispute," in red. This is what I need, I didn't think you had to swipe the row specifically, not the card itself.

claim: Snag 5: the card screen has no "Limits" option, the participant can't find it among the details and history
quote: Besides the details and history, I don't see anything here that looks like limits. Maybe it's in the card's own settings, not in the general section.

claim: Snag 6: the limit slider has too coarse a step, the exact value of $50 can't be set
quote: For some reason it jumps straight to zero or to $100, I can't get it exactly to $50, the step is too big.

claim: Snag 7: the SMS code input field is visually indistinguishable from static text, mistaken for a freeze
quote: Entering the code... so, where do I type it, there's just an empty line here with no placeholder, at first I thought this was a bug and the screen had frozen.

claim: The participant expects that opening the card will immediately show the list of recent charges on it
quote: Weird, I thought that if you open the card, it would immediately show the list of recent charges on it.

claim: The participant expects a separate field for typing the exact limit number next to the slider
quote: I thought you could type in the exact number by hand, not just drag with your finger. Like in most apps — either a slider, or a text field next to it.

claim: The participant doesn't immediately notice the "History" item on the card screen, finds it after scrolling down
quote: Scrolling down... ah, here at the bottom it says "History" in small print. Why isn't this at the top?

claim: The participant switches to the support chat, but the 24-hour SLA doesn't fit the urgent task, then goes back and finds the function via a swipe
quote: The bot writes: "Please describe in detail, an agent will respond within 24 hours." Twenty-four hours is a long time, if money was just fraudulently taken from me right now.

claim: The dispute request is submitted and confirmed with a request number
quote: A message came in — "Request #558212 accepted, status can be tracked in the Requests section." Great, got that sorted, although it took longer than I expected.

claim: The limit is changed and confirmed by the system, but the participant is not sure of the exact final amount
quote: It says — "Limit Changed." Good, I guess, but I'm not entirely sure it's exactly $50 and not $52 or something like that, the scale is too coarse to check by eye.

claim: Annoyance over the single irrelevant "Repeat Payment" button on the transaction card
quote: Overall, honestly, it was a bit annoying that the "Repeat Payment" button on the card is the only one — that's really confusing, it seems like nothing at all is provided here for disputed transactions.

claim: Relief/satisfaction at the moment of finding the dispute function via the swipe
quote: Oh! There it is, it appeared — "Dispute," in red. This is what I need

claim: CONCLUSION: the dominant problem type in the session is FINDABILITY (5 out of 7 snags)
quote: I didn't think you had to swipe the row specifically, not the card itself

claim: The barrier on the path to the "Dispute" function is SLOWING: the task was completed, but only after several wrong attempts
quote: Great, got that sorted, although it took longer than I expected.

claim: The barrier from the limit slider's step is unstable between SLOWING and COSMETIC, since the accuracy of the final amount is not confirmed by the participant
quote: I'm not entirely sure it's exactly $50 and not $52 or something like that, the scale is too coarse to check by eye

claim: Gap: the participant expected a field for exact number entry next to the limit slider, but the interface only has a coarse slider with no input field
quote: I thought you could type in the exact number by hand, not just drag with your finger. Like in most apps — either a slider, or a text field next to it.

claim: Cannot reliably separate an architectural interface problem from the participant's individual habit — she attributes part of the snag to her own unfamiliarity with the swipe gesture, not an interface defect
quote: I'm just not used to swiping list rows at all, I usually tap directly on the entry itself

claim: Highest fix priority is the path to the "Dispute" function, since it's tied to urgency and nearly led to using an unacceptably slow channel (24-hour SLA)
quote: Twenty-four hours is a long time, if money was just fraudulently taken from me right now.

claim: Main insight: at the step of searching for the dispute function, the participant hit a path-blocking (but not outcome-blocking) findability barrier, severity SLOWING
quote: I didn't think you had to swipe the row specifically, not the card itself
